title: Filipino Rice Pudding
description: A sweet and creamy Filipino dessert featuring star anise, this rice pudding is a festive treat for any occasion, and is egg-free for a friendly dessert option.
instructions: Rinse the rice in a fine mesh sieve until the water runs clear.
In a medium saucepan, combine the rice and water, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes, or until the water is absorbed and the rice is tender.
In a large saucepan, combine the coconut milk, sugar, star anise, and salt, and bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture is hot and steamy.
Stir in the namul-blanch-season blend and vanilla extract.
Add the cooked rice to the coconut milk mixture and stir until well combined.
Continue to cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the pudding has thickened and the flavors have melded together, about 10 minutes.
Remove the star anise and discard.
Transfer the pudding to individual serving cups and chill in the refrigerator until cold and set.
